basics.name: Manuel Sánchez Hernández
basics.label: AI and Machine Learning Leader
basics.summary: AI and machine learning leader focused on turning complex technology into measurable business outcomes. Most recently Engineering Director of Machine Learning at Adevinta, where I led central ML capabilities and worked across AI strategy, platforms, computer vision, generative AI, and organizational enablement. Earlier experience spans Schibsted, Morgan Stanley, and Procter & Gamble, plus teaching and facilitation for executive and technical audiences.

work.name: Adevinta
work.summary: Most recently led Adevinta central machine learning capabilities, combining engineering leadership, AI strategy, platform thinking, and organizational change across European marketplaces.

work.name: Adevinta
work.position: Head of Product of Machine Learning Services Area
work.summary: Co-led Adevinta Machine Learning Services, defining product vision, strategy, stakeholder alignment, and execution for global ML teams serving the company marketplaces. The area built shared capabilities that helped product teams adopt machine learning more easily, including ML platforms, computer vision services, internal education, and community programs.

work.name: Different universities
work.position: Invited teacher / learning facilitator on machine learning
work.summary: Teach and facilitate applied machine learning and AI sessions for executive and technical audiences, including programs connected to MIT, University of Chicago, ESADE, and Universitat Pompeu Fabra. The focus is helping leaders and practitioners understand how ML is built, scaled, and translated into business value.

work.name: Schibsted Media Group
work.position: Engineering Manager / Staff Data Scientist, ML Enablement
work.summary: Built and scaled machine learning adoption across Schibsted marketplaces, working with product and engineering teams on applied ML use cases in search, acquisition, user experience, and trust. Started as a hands-on data scientist and evolved toward enablement leadership, creating workshops, reusable practices, and a team model that helped more teams use ML effectively.

work.name: IE Business School
work.position: Co-writer of textbook - Machine Learning and Big Data
work.summary: Co-authored the Machine Learning and Big Data section of an academic textbook used in economic intelligence programs at several Spanish universities. The work translated applied ML concepts for business, strategy, and decision-making audiences.

work.name: Self-Employed
work.position: Machine learning business and technical consultant
work.summary: Independent consultant helping financial and technology companies apply quantitative research, machine learning, and business analytics to strategic decisions. Work included quantitative strategy development, sales analytics, and interim leadership during a post-acquisition business transition.

work.name: Morgan Stanley
work.summary: Worked in the front-office equity derivatives team developing quantitative investment strategies, portfolio construction tools, and analytics for trading desks and clients. The role combined statistical modeling, financial product thinking, and client-facing support for systematic investment solutions.

work.name: Arbora & Ausonia (Procter & Gamble JV)
work.position: Product Manager and Production Planning Manager
work.summary: Started in production planning and later moved into product management in P&G Iberian joint venture, Arbora & Ausonia. Led cross-functional and international work across supply, marketing, finance, and commercial teams, including product launches, operational planning, and brand-profit improvement.

volunteer.summary: Mentoring of students. I look to inspire and help them by communicating positive values and speak about the implications of their own career decisions.

awards.summary: As product manager in P&G, awarded best spot in international advertisement festival (2010), Best Social Campaign 2008 (internal P&G prize), Best Press Campaign given by national magazine (2008), Best Advertisement of P&G Marketing Communication Seminar (2007).

awards.title: National Physics Olympics
awards.summary: Fourth prize in the Spanish National Physics Olympics.

publications.name: Escape of photons from two fixed extreme Reissner-Nordström black holes
publications.summary: We study the scattering of light (null geodesics) by two fixed extreme Reissner-Nordstr\"om black holes, in which the gravitational attraction of their masses is exactly balanced with the electrostatic repulsion of their charges, allowing a static spacetime. We identify the set of unstable periodic orbits that constitute the fractal repeller that completely describes the chaotic escape dynamics of photons. In the framework of periodic orbit theory, the analysis of the linear stability of the unstable periodic orbits is used to obtain the main quantities of chaos that characterize the escape dynamics of the photons scattered by the black holes. In particular, the escape rate which is compared with the result obtained from numerical simulations that consider statistical ensembles of photons. We also analyze the dynamics of photons in the proximity of a perturbed black hole and give an analytical estimation for the escape rate in this system.

projects.name: TheorIA Dataset
projects.summary: Open dataset of theoretical physics results and derivations designed to support evaluation, fine-tuning, and benchmarking of language models on physics reasoning tasks.

projects.name: Pragmatic Evaluation of Software Engineering AI Tooling
projects.summary: Enterprise AI-coding-tool evaluation across 77 engineers, 14 teams, and 165 real tasks, comparing GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Claude Code.
